codeReview.summary: The reactive modal wiring, ARIA surface, and FIFO queue scaffolding are substantial, but key contract behaviors are not fully met: per-prompt TTL handling breaks across queued prompts, multi-tab policy sync is only dismissal sync, and "Always allow" is not persisted across lock/unlock.

issues.description: `remainingMs` is initialized once from `initialRemaining` and never reset when `event.id` changes. Because `DashboardScreen` reuses the same modal instance for FIFO queue advancement (`src/screens/DashboardScreen/index.tsx:814` has no key), the next queued prompt inherits stale/zero TTL from the previous prompt and can auto-dismiss immediately via the `remainingMs <= 0` effect (`PolicyPromptModal.tsx:157-160`), violating per-prompt TTL/FIFO behavior.

Multi-tab propagation only broadcasts `{ type: 'policy-resolved', id }` and remote tabs only remove the queue item (`AppStateProvider.tsx:2009-2017`); no policy decision or peer/method payload is propagated, and remote tabs do not call `setPolicyOverride`. This does not satisfy the expected behavior that an "Always allow" action in tab A is reflected in tab B within a runtime-status tick.

"Always allow" is applied only to the current in-memory runtime via `setPolicyOverride`, but lock/unlock recreates runtime state from the stored encrypted profile (`unlockProfile` decodes persisted package at `AppStateProvider.tsx:1783-1796`) while `lockProfile` discards the runtime (`AppStateProvider.tsx:1836-1861`) without persisting updated overrides. This fails the expected behavior that "Always allow" persists across lock/unlock.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill-procedure adherence appears over-reported for this UI-changing feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `ui-screen-worker` marks agent-browser verification as required for changed screens/modals and says omissions must set `followedProcedure: false` with deviations (`.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:23,51-53`), but the handoff reports `followedProcedure: true` with empty deviations and only CLI verification commands (`.../handoffs/2026-04-21T20-24-40-911Z__m2-reactive-policy-prompt-modal__79012a27-1aee-42bb-a253-58973bb56c17.json:14-53`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Runtime-deviation documentation has drifted from implementation details for allow-once rollback semantics.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The deviation doc says lock rollback uses `setPolicyOverride(peer, "deny")` (`docs/runtime-deviations-from-paper.md:134`), while implementation rolls back with `value: "unset"` (`src/app/AppStateProvider.tsx:1849-1853`).

summary: Review result: FAIL. The feature contains substantial implementation work, but blocking contract gaps remain around per-prompt TTL queue behavior, true multi-tab policy synchronization, and lock/unlock persistence for "Always allow" overrides.
